text: Which is the largest land animal?
explanation: The largest land animal is the African elephant.

text: How many legs does a spider have?
explanation: All spiders have eight legs.

text: Which bird cannot fly but runs fast?
explanation: The ostrich cannot fly but runs at great speed.

text: Which animal is called the 'king of the beasts'?
explanation: The lion is traditionally called the king of the beasts.

text: What covers the body of most fish?
explanation: A fish's body is usually covered with scales.

text: Which domestic animal meows?
explanation: The cat meows.

text: Which is the tallest animal in the world?
explanation: The tallest animal is the giraffe.

text: Which of these is an insect?
explanation: The insect in the list is the butterfly (it has six legs).

text: Which animal do people most often milk for dairy?
explanation: The main source of milk for people is the cow.

text: Which animal lives in water and breathes with gills?
explanation: Fish breathe using gills.

text: How many legs do insects have?
explanation: All insects have six legs.

text: Which is the fastest animal on land?
explanation: The cheetah is the fastest land animal.

text: Which of these is an amphibian?
explanation: The frog is an amphibian; the others are reptiles.

text: What does a panda mainly eat?
explanation: The giant panda feeds almost entirely on bamboo.

text: Which animal can change the colour of its skin to match its surroundings?
explanation: The chameleon can change its colouring.

text: What is a baby dog called?
explanation: A baby dog is a puppy.

text: Which mammal is capable of true flight?
explanation: Bats are the only mammals capable of true flight (the flying squirrel only glides).

text: Who builds honeycombs and makes honey?
explanation: Honeycombs and honey are made by bees.

text: Which animal carries its young in a pouch on its belly?
explanation: The kangaroo carries its young in a pouch.

text: How many humps does a dromedary camel have?
explanation: A dromedary has a single hump.

text: Which is the largest animal on Earth?
explanation: The blue whale is the largest animal that has ever lived.

text: To which class do animals that nurse their young with milk belong?
explanation: Animals that feed their young milk are mammals.

text: How many chambers does the heart of most mammals have?
explanation: Mammals have a four-chambered heart.

text: Which of these is an egg-laying mammal?
explanation: The platypus is one of the few mammals that lay eggs.

text: What is a group of wolves that live and hunt together called?
explanation: A group of wolves is called a pack.

text: What is a group of lions called?
explanation: A family group of lions is called a pride.

text: Which is the longest snake in the world?
explanation: The longest snake is the reticulated python.

text: How many hearts does an octopus have?
explanation: An octopus has three hearts.

text: Which of these sea animals is a fish, not a mammal?
explanation: The shark is a fish; the dolphin, whale and seal are mammals.

text: How long can giant tortoises live?
explanation: Giant tortoises can live more than a hundred years.

text: What is the transformation of a caterpillar into a butterfly called?
explanation: The complete transformation of an insect is called metamorphosis.

text: What is the science of birds called?
explanation: Birds are studied by ornithology.

text: Which animal stores fat in its hump and can go a long time without water in the desert?
explanation: The fat stored in its hump helps the camel cope with a lack of water.

text: To which class do snakes and lizards belong?
explanation: Snakes and lizards are reptiles.

text: Which mammal is considered the slowest?
explanation: The slowest mammal is the sloth.

text: What do herbivorous animals eat?
explanation: Herbivores feed on plant matter.

text: Which bird is active at night and is a symbol of wisdom?
explanation: A nocturnal lifestyle and 'wisdom' are associated with the owl.

text: What is a baby horse called?
explanation: A baby horse is a foal.

text: Which striped black-and-white animal is related to the horse?
explanation: The zebra is a striped relative of the horse.

text: Which mammal navigates using echolocation?
explanation: Bats use echolocation to navigate and hunt.

text: Which animal has the largest heart of any living creature?
explanation: The blue whale has the largest heart, weighing hundreds of kilograms.

text: How many compartments does a cow's stomach have?
explanation: A cow's stomach has four compartments.

text: Which animal can regrow lost limbs?
explanation: The axolotl can regenerate limbs and even parts of organs.

text: Which bird lays the largest eggs?
explanation: The ostrich lays the largest eggs of any living bird.

text: What is a male chicken called?
explanation: A male chicken is a rooster.

text: Which animal has compound eyes made of thousands of separate lenses?
explanation: A dragonfly's compound eyes are made of thousands of units.

text: To which order of mammals does the dolphin belong?
explanation: Dolphins belong to the order of cetaceans.

text: Which fish can produce a strong electric discharge?
explanation: The electric eel generates a discharge for hunting and defence.

text: What is a group of fish swimming together called?
explanation: A group of fish swimming together is called a school.

text: Which animal has the longest neck?
explanation: The longest neck belongs to the giraffe.

text: How many legs does a crab have?
explanation: Crabs are decapod crustaceans, so they have ten legs.

text: Which of these animals can sleep standing up?
explanation: A horse can doze standing thanks to a special leg structure.

text: What is the science of insects called?
explanation: Insects are studied by entomology.

text: Which is the largest land carnivore?
explanation: The largest land carnivore is the polar bear.

text: Which method of orientation using sound do dolphins use?
explanation: Dolphins orient themselves using echolocation.

text: Which of these animals is a marsupial?
explanation: The koala is a marsupial mammal.

text: What is a baby sheep called?
explanation: A baby sheep is a lamb.

text: Which bird can turn its head almost 270 degrees?
explanation: Owls can turn their heads almost 270° thanks to a special neck structure.

text: Which is the most venomous land snake by venom strength?
explanation: The inland taipan has the most potent venom of any land snake.

text: What is a baby bear called?
explanation: A baby bear is called a cub.

text: What is the name for a male bee that comes from an unfertilized egg?
explanation: The correct answer is Drone.

text: Snakes and lizards are known to flick their tongue, this behavior is to?
explanation: The correct answer is Capture scent particles.

text: Which of the following is NOT a venomous snake?
explanation: The correct answer is Reticulated python.

text: For what reason would a spotted hyena "laugh"?
explanation: The correct answer is Nervousness.

text: What colour is the female blackbird?
explanation: The correct answer is Brown.

text: What are the scales on all snakes and most lizards are made of?
explanation: The correct answer is Keratin.

text: Which dog breed is named after a region in Croatia
explanation: The correct answer is Dalmatian.

text: Which species of Brown Bear is not extinct?
explanation: The correct answer is Syrian Brown Bear.

text: What is the fastest  land animal?
explanation: The correct answer is Cheetah.

text: What type of animal is a natterjack?
explanation: The correct answer is Toad.

text: What was the name of the Ethiopian Wolf before they knew it was related to wolves?
explanation: The correct answer is Simien Jackel.

text: By definition, where does an abyssopelagic animal live?
options: At the bottom of the ocean
explanation: The correct answer is At the bottom of the ocean.

text: What is the Gray Wolf's scientific name?
explanation: The correct answer is Canis Lupus.

text: What is the scientific name of the Common Chimpanzee?
explanation: The correct answer is Pan troglodytes.

text: Which of the following is true when alligators are behaving territorially?
options: They bellow while showing their tail and neck
Open their jaws while making a clicking noise
They run full force at the threat
Slap their tails on the ground
explanation: The correct answer is They bellow while showing their tail and neck.

text: What is the scientific name for modern day humans?
explanation: The correct answer is Homo Sapiens.

text: Which is the national animal of India?
explanation: The correct answer is Tiger.

text: A carnivorous animal eats flesh, what does a nucivorous animal eat?
explanation: The correct answer is Nuts.

text: What is the collective noun for vultures?
explanation: The correct answer is Wake.

text: To which biological phylum do all mammals, birds and reptiles belong?
explanation: The correct answer is Chordata.

text: Which of the following is another name for the "Poecilotheria Metallica Tarantula"?
explanation: The correct answer is Gooty.

text: Cashmere is the wool from which kind of animal?
explanation: The correct answer is Goat.

text: What is the collective noun for bears?
explanation: The correct answer is Sloth.

text: How many known living species of hyenas are there?
explanation: The correct answer is 4.

text: What is the name of a rabbit's abode?
explanation: The correct answer is Burrow.

text: About how much of their body weight in food do sea otters eat every day?
explanation: The correct answer is 25%.

text: Which of these is a colony of polyps and not a jellyfish?
explanation: The correct answer is Portuguese Man-of-War.

text: What is the collective noun for a group of crows?
explanation: The correct answer is Murder.

text: What is the name of the family that the domestic cat is a member of?
explanation: The correct answer is Felidae.

text: What is the average lifespan of a domestic rabbit?
explanation: The correct answer is 8-12 years.

text: What is the average amount of chinchillas born into a single litter?
explanation: The correct answer is 2-3.

text: What type of creature is a Bonobo?
explanation: The correct answer is Ape.

text: What is the name of the copper-rich protein that creates the blue blood in the Antarctic octopus?
explanation: The correct answer is Hemocyanin.

text: What are rhino's horn made of?
explanation: The correct answer is Keratin.

text: What is the world's longest venomous snake?
explanation: The correct answer is King Cobra.

text: Which of these species is not extinct?
explanation: The correct answer is Komodo dragon.

text: Which species is a "mountain chicken"?
explanation: The correct answer is Frog.

text: Which of these animals are not real?
explanation: The correct answer is Nemean Lion.

text: Unlike on most salamanders, this part of a newt is flat?
explanation: The correct answer is Tail.

text: Which breed of dog is traditionally associated with firefighters?
explanation: The correct answer is Dalmatians.

text: Which of these animals is NOT a lizard?
explanation: The correct answer is Tuatara.

text: What is the national bird of Bahrain?
explanation: The correct answer is Bulbul.

text: What is the scientific name of the cheetah?
explanation: The correct answer is Acinonyx jubatus.

text: What is the collective noun for rats?
explanation: The correct answer is Mischief.

text: What do you call a baby bat?
explanation: The correct answer is Pup.

text: How many teeth does an adult rabbit have?
explanation: The correct answer is 28.

text: Hippocampus is the Latin name for which marine creature?
explanation: The correct answer is Seahorse.

text: The Kākāpō is a large, flightless, nocturnal parrot native to which country?
explanation: The correct answer is New Zealand.

text: The dish Fugu, is made from what family of fish?
explanation: The correct answer is Pufferfish.

text: "Decapods" are an order of ten-footed crustaceans.  Which of these are NOT decapods?
explanation: The correct answer is Krill.

text: What is the scientific name for the "Polar Bear"?
explanation: The correct answer is Ursus Maritimus.

text: What scientific suborder does the family Hyaenidae belong to?
explanation: The correct answer is Feliformia.

text: What color/colour is a polar bear's skin?
explanation: The correct answer is Black.

text: What is the common term for bovine spongiform encephalopathy (BSE)?
explanation: The correct answer is Mad Cow disease.

text: What scientific family does the Aardwolf belong to?
explanation: The correct answer is Hyaenidae.

text: What is the fastest animal?
explanation: The correct answer is Peregrine Falcon.

text: What is the scientific name of the Budgerigar?
explanation: The correct answer is Melopsittacus undulatus.

text: What is the scientific name for the Bald Eagle?
explanation: The correct answer is Haliaeetus Leucocephalus .

text: What dog breed is one of the oldest breeds of dog and has flourished since before 400 BCE.
explanation: The correct answer is Pugs.

text: Which class of animals are newts members of?
explanation: The correct answer is Amphibian.

text: Which sea animal is the largest mammal (and the largest animal overall) on Earth?
explanation: A blue whale can weigh over 150 tons and reach up to 30 meters in length.

text: Which land mammal of the cat family is the fastest runner in the world?
explanation: A cheetah can accelerate to speeds of 100-120 km/h in short sprints.

text: Which tiny bird is the only one in the world capable of flying backward and hovering in mid-air?
explanation: A hummingbird's wings beat up to 80 times per second, tracing a figure-eight pattern.

text: On which islands did Charles Darwin study finches, which helped him formulate the theory of evolution?
explanation: Darwin observed differences in the beak shapes of finches based on their diets on different islands.

text: To which unusual order of egg-laying mammals does the platypus belong?
explanation: The platypus and the echidna are the only surviving representatives of the monotremes order.

text: By which complex behavior ('dance') do honeybees convey information to hive mates about the distance and direction to flowers?
explanation: The waggle dance of bees was deciphered and detailed by Austrian ethologist Karl von Frisch.

text: Which insect makes annual flights of thousands of miles from Canada and the US to Mexico in groups of millions?
explanation: The monarch butterfly is famous for its grand annual multi-generational migration.

text: Which animal actually builds coral reefs, functioning as a tiny marine invertebrate?
explanation: Coral polyps secrete calcium carbonate to build a hard protective exoskeleton.

text: Which unique amphibian is capable of fully regenerating lost limbs, tail, and even parts of its heart?
explanation: The axolotl (ambystoma larva) is an essential model organism in regenerative biology.

text: Which microscopic invertebrate ('water bear') is capable of surviving open space, heavy radiation, and temperatures near absolute zero?
explanation: Tardigrades enter a state of cryptobiosis (anhydrobiosis), suspending their metabolism.

text: Which class of cephalopod mollusks possesses the most advanced active camouflage system, changing skin color and texture in milliseconds?
explanation: Cuttlefish control chromatophores directly via their nervous system to blend in instantly.

text: What is the highest level of social organization in animals, characterized by division of labor and cooperative care of offspring?
explanation: Eusociality is observed in ants, termites, certain bees, and naked mole-rats.
